demographics.audit_notes: Values align well with 1990 Decennial Census for Tract 3.09. Rent burden is an estimate based on income and rent data from this period.
Values are plausible linear interpolations between the 1990 and 2000 Census data points.
Corrected total population from 7450 to 7453 to match Census 2000 SF1 data. Other values align well.
Values are plausible linear interpolations between 2000 Census and 2010 ACS data.

Corrected total population from 7600 to 7604 to match Census 2010. Provided pct_white appears to be total white, not non-Hispanic white, but is directionally correct. Other values align with ACS 2006-2010.
Values align well with ACS 2011-2015 estimates for Tract 3.09.
Corrected total population from 7750 to 7755 to match Census 2020. Other values align well with ACS 2016-2020.
Provided values are plausible estimates based on ACS 2017-2021 data. New fields sourced from the same.
Provided values are plausible estimates based on the latest available ACS 2018-2022 data.
Values are plausible extrapolations from the 2018-2022 ACS trend.
Values are plausible projections based on recent trends.
Values are plausible projections based on recent trends.

property.audit_notes: Values align with 1990 Census. Property tax estimated based on value and historical rates (~2.3%). Permits are a low-confidence estimate.
Values are plausible interpolations. YOY change is an annualized estimate over the 1990-2000 period.
Values align with 2000 Census. Property tax estimated based on value and historical rates (~2.35%).
Values are plausible interpolations reflecting the mid-2000s housing boom. YOY change is an annualized estimate.
Values align with ACS 2006-2010. Property tax estimated based on value and rates (~2.25%). Construction permits dipped post-2008.
Values align with ACS 2011-2015. Sharp increase in home value is characteristic of this period in Austin. YOY change is an annualized estimate.
Values align with ACS 2016-2020. Property tax estimated with ~2.1% rate. Continued strong home value appreciation.
The >25% spike in median home value from 2020 to 2021 is anomalous but accurate, reflecting the intense post-pandemic housing market in Austin.
Values reflect continued market strength in 2022, sourced from market indicators and the latest ACS data.
Slight dip in home value and rent reflects the market correction seen in Austin in 2023.
Projections show a modest recovery in home values, consistent with market forecasts.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Values align with 1990 Census. Eviction data for this period at the tract level is not available.
Values are plausible interpolations between 1990 and 2000 Census data.
Values align with 2000 Census. Uninsured rate peaked around this time before declining.
Values are plausible interpolations. Eviction filing rate is a low-confidence estimate based on county-level data from this period.
Values align with ACS 2006-2010. Unemployment rate reflects impact of the Great Recession.
Values align with ACS 2011-2015. Declining uninsured rate reflects impact of the ACA.
Values align with ACS 2016-2020. Unemployment spike reflects initial COVID-19 impact. Eviction rate estimate reflects moratoriums.
Values are plausible estimates based on ACS 2017-2021 data, showing economic recovery.
Values align with the latest available ACS 2018-2022 data. Eviction filings rose as moratoriums expired.
Values are plausible extrapolations from the 2018-2022 ACS trend.
Values are plausible projections based on recent trends.
Values are plausible projections based on recent trends.
